answers: 3D printing builds objects layer by layer from digital files. FDM (filament) is most affordable, Resin gives finer detail.
Beginner printers: Creality Ender 3 (~$200), Bambu Lab A1 (~$400). Both reliable with huge communities.
Process: design in CAD (or download from Thingiverse) → slice with Cura → print → post-process.
3D printing tips: level your bed, use proper temperature, add supports for overhangs, and start with simple models.
